title: Racosperma truculentum
taxonomy: Racosperma truculentum is a species of shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The genus Racosperma was previously considered a synonym of Acacia, but is now recognized as a distinct genus. Racosperma truculentum is classified as a member of the genus Racosperma and the subgenus Phyllodinae. Its classification is also part of the larger plant classification system used in the APG III system.
uses: Racosperma truculentum, also known as the prickly acacia, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Its leaves and branches are also used in traditional medicine to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In addition, the plant's bark is believed to have antiseptic properties, which can aid in the healing of cuts and sores. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Racosperma truculentum's medicinal uses.

side_effects: Racosperma truculentum, commonly known as the 'Prickly Acacia', has been reported to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its spines and sap.
